title: Conjecture 1.1 (Elphick–Farber–Goldberg–Wocjan)
statement_text: For every connected graph $G$ of order $n$, we have $$\min\{s^{+}(G),s^{-}(G)\}\geq n-1.$$
context_text: Elphick, Farber, Goldberg and Wocjan conjectured in 2016 that the positive and negative square energies of any connected graph both exceed $n-1$. It has been verified for hyper-energetic graphs, regular graphs, and other families, and the best unconditional lower bound prior to this paper was $\frac{3n}{4}$.
paper_contribution: Verifies the conjecture for all connected graphs with domination number at most 2, and proves the stronger Conjecture 1.2 (implying Conjecture 1.1) for claw-free graphs and for graphs with diameter 2.
paper_title: Refinement of a conjecture on positive square energy of graphs

title: Conjecture 1.2
statement_text: Let $G$ be a connected graph of order $n$ and size $m$. If $m\geq n+1$, then $$s^{+}(G)\geq n.$$
context_text: Motivated by the difficulty of proving Conjecture 1.1 inductively (because the bound $n-1$ is not preserved under subgraph removal), the authors ask when the strictly stronger bound $s^{+}(G)\geq n$ holds and propose this conjecture after computational investigation. It has been verified for all connected graphs up to 9 vertices and all graphs in the Mathematica database with $n\leq 100$ and $m\geq n+1$.
paper_title: Refinement of a conjecture on positive square energy of graphs

title: Conjecture 9.1
statement_text: If $G$ is a unicyclic graph of order $n$ whose cycle has odd length $k$, then $s^{+}(G)>n>s^{-}(G)$, if $k\equiv 3\mod 4$. $s^{+}(G)<n<s^{-}(G)$, if $k\equiv 1\mod 4$.
context_text: The authors compute $s^{+}$ and $s^{-}$ for odd cycles $C_n$ explicitly and observe a parity-of-$n$-mod-4 dependence. They extend this observation to all unicyclic graphs whose unique cycle has odd length and propose this conjecture.
paper_title: Refinement of a conjecture on positive square energy of graphs

title: Conjecture 9.2
statement_text: Let $G$ be a connected graph of order $n$. $s^{+}(G)=n-1$ if and only if $G$ is a tree. $s^{-}(G)=n-1$ if and only if $G$ is a tree or a complete graph.
context_text: After establishing Conjecture 1.1 for several families, the authors propose a characterisation of the equality case $s^{+}(G)=n-1$ (resp. $s^{-}(G)=n-1$) in Conjecture 1.1.
paper_title: Refinement of a conjecture on positive square energy of graphs

title: Conjecture 9.3
statement_text: Let $G$ be a connected graph of order $n$ such that $s^{+}(G)=n$. Then $G$ is a bipartite unicyclic graph.
context_text: The authors believe equality never holds in Conjecture 1.2 for non-unicyclic graphs, and propose this characterisation of the equality case $s^{+}(G)=n$ based on computational evidence and the structure of bipartite unicyclic graphs.
paper_title: Refinement of a conjecture on positive square energy of graphs

title: Conjecture 9.4
statement_text: For any connected graph $G$ of order $n$ and $\omega(G)\geq 3$, we have $s^{+}(G)\geq n$.
context_text: This conjecture refines Conjecture 1.1 for graphs with clique number at least 3, motivated by the expectation that a large clique forces a large value of $s^{+}$. It would follow from the truth of Conjectures 1.2 and 9.1; the authors identify unicyclic graphs with a triangle as the main obstacle.
paper_title: Refinement of a conjecture on positive square energy of graphs

title: Conjecture 9.5
statement_text: The following hold: Let $G$ be a maximal planar graph of order $n\geq 10$. Then $s^{+}(G)\geq 3n$. Let $G$ be a maximal outerplanar graph of order $n\geq 8$. Then $s^{+}(G)\geq 2n$.
context_text: Elphick and Linz noted that Conjecture 1.1 holds for maximal planar graphs and asked whether $s^{+}(G)\geq 3(n-2)$ for such graphs. The present authors propose the stronger bounds $3n$ and $2n$ for maximal planar and maximal outerplanar graphs respectively, showing the coefficients are asymptotically tight via explicit constructions.
paper_title: Refinement of a conjecture on positive square energy of graphs
